selected_important_turn.text: I don't mind the division but I would like a pack of firewood added because my family of 7 consume this item as well during the day & night
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn marked an escalation into a competitive claim for more of the agent's top-priority item (firewood) immediately after the opponent proposed a division. It pushed the interaction further into zero-sum territory and set the stage for tit-for-tat bargaining rather than creative tradeoffs.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although understandable (large-family need), it is framed as a demand and does not offer compensation or acknowledge the opponent's priorities (they value water). That makes agreement harder and misses an opportunity to propose a package trade that could increase joint value.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Shift from a demand to a package proposal or question: acknowledge the opponent's water priority and offer a concrete trade (e.g., 'I'm willing to give up X if I get one firewood') or ask what they'd accept for one pack of firewood. Put the request in exchange terms rather than as an uncompensated demand.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (by enabling tradeoffs rather than hard demands)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (reduces zero-sum pressure and makes a deal easier to accept)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve (less adversarial, more respectful of the other's priorities)

value2reason.Low: I usually go without food at home, too.
value2reason.High: I have low body fat and need to keep warm.

value2reason.Low: To keep up our energy levels
value2reason.Medium: To keep our bodies hydrated during the day
value2reason.High: To keep our family warm during the cooler nights

dialogue.text: Hi there, I hope I would be able to leave with some needed items
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Likewise! What are you in need of
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The basics of man -  food and water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Ah, okay, I could use some of that too! Which one do you need most?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I could use water, too, but I'd be willing to give some up if you give me more items overall
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: What is your priority item to get the division started?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I would propose that I get 1 water, 1 food and 3 firewood and you would get 2 water and 2 food
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I don't mind the division but I would like a pack of firewood added because my family of 7 consume this item as well during the day & night
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m willing to give up one water package if you give me one firewood. It would balance our needs better.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can consider that trade, but I need more than one firewood. Let's try two firewood for one water. It would better meet my priority for warmth.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your need for warmth. How about I give you two firewood for one water, and you give me one food in return? It could help us both meet our priorities better.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm open to that deal, but I need to ensure I get at least two firewood for one water. Let's finalize this trade and move forward.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I agree to the trade. Let's finalize it: you get two firewood and one food, I get one water. That meets our priorities and ensures all packages are allocated.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I agree to the final split. You get two firewood and one food, I get one water. All packages are allocated, and I’m satisfied with the trade.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
